commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the patch looks mechanically correct and the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the generated schema object-shape compatibility risk.; Cleared: The diff hardens generated config schema assembly and does not add dependencies, permissions, secrets handling, network calls, or code execution surfaces. (sha=ef08cd1e64fd67119aedbab9f48bd3581c11e46d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes dynamic plugin/channel config-schema property maps to null-prototype records, writes extension ids with safe data-property semantics, and adds a regression test for `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` ids.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +52. Total +105 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main writes dynamic plugin and channel ids into ordinary `properties` records with bracket assignment, so `__proto__` is not preserved as an own schema entry. I did not run tests in this read-only review, but the PR body includes a before/after runtime schema probe for the exact PR head.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the patch looks mechanically correct and the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the generated schema object-shape compatibility risk.

target.title: fix: harden config schema extension id maps [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: generated config schemas wrote plugin and channel ids directly into JSON schema `properties` maps.
- Solution: normalize those dynamic schema property maps to null-prototype records and add entries with safe data-property semantics.
- What changed: added schema-map helpers for plugin/channel config schema assembly and a regression test for object-special extension ids.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): extension id normalization, config defaults, lookup path restrictions, and ordinary plugin/channel schema output shape are unchanged.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Motivation

- Generated config schema output should stay structurally stable when extension metadata contains object-special key names.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- N/A in public PR metadata.
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated config schema property maps safely preserve plugin and channel ids that match object-special key names as inert own schema entries.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node v22.22.2; before-fix comparison used current main cdb55b3edb371edd2a4ba7b0a035a80e38497a60, and after-fix proof used exact PR head ef08cd1e64fd67119aedbab9f48bd3581c11e46d.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Same OpenClaw runtime schema probe was run on current main first, then on the exact PR head.
git switch --detach cdb55b3edb371edd2a4ba7b0a035a80e38497a60
OPENCLAW_PROOF_LABEL=main OPENCLAW_PROOF_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<runtime schema probe>'

git switch --detach ef08cd1e64fd67119aedbab9f48bd3581c11e46d
OPENCLAW_PROOF_LABEL=pr-84128 OPENCLAW_PROOF_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<same runtime schema probe>'
```

Evidence after fix: Copied sanitized console output from the runtime schema probe. The main output is included first as the before-fix comparison; the PR-head output follows as after-fix proof.
```text
main before fix:
{
  "label": "main",
  "gitSha": "cdb55b3edb37",
  "pluginPropertiesPrototype": "Object.prototype",
  "pluginEntries": {
    "__proto__": {
      "ownProperty": false,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": false
    },
    "constructor": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "prototype": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    }
  },
  "channelPropertiesPrototype": "custom-schema-object",
  "channelEntries": {
    "__proto__": {
      "ownProperty": false,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "constructor": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "prototype": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    }
  }
}

PR head after fix:
{
  "label": "pr-84128",
  "gitSha": "ef08cd1e64fd",
  "pluginPropertiesPrototype": "null",
  "pluginEntries": {
    "__proto__": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "constructor": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "prototype": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    }
  },
  "channelPropertiesPrototype": "null",
  "channelEntries": {
    "__proto__": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "constructor": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    },
    "prototype": {
      "ownProperty": true,
      "tokenSchemaReachable": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: On main, `__proto__` is not emitted as an own plugin/channel schema entry and the channel property map prototype becomes a schema object. On the PR head, plugin and channel property maps have null prototypes, and `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` are all own entries with reachable schema fields.

What was not tested: Full suite, packaged build, live gateway startup, and an installed third-party plugin using these ids; this proof directly exercised the OpenClaw runtime schema builder with plugin/channel metadata shaped like runtime discovery input.
## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: dynamic extension ids were inserted into ordinary JSON schema `properties` objects with bracket assignment.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression coverage for extension ids that overlap object-special key names.
- Contributing context (if known): schema merge cloning can turn a previously normalized property map back into an ordinary object, so final schema assembly also needs normalization.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: plugin and channel ids `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` remain own entries in generated schema property maps without changing those maps' prototypes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `buildConfigSchema` owns the property-map generation path, so a direct schema test covers both plugin and channel insertion without unrelated runtime setup.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Generated config schema output now safely includes plugin/channel ids matching object-special key names as own properties. No config defaults or migrations changed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local worktree
- Runtime/container: repository Vitest wrapper in local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): generated plugin/channel config schema metadata
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Generate a config schema with plugin and channel ids named `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype`.
2. Inspect the generated plugin and channel schema `properties` maps.
3. Run `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Dynamic schema maps keep inert prototypes and store each special id as an own schema property.

### Actual

- The focused config schema test passed with all 45 tests green.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused config schema test passed after the change; regression checks plugin and channel ids for all three object-special names.
- Edge cases checked: own-property presence, null-prototype maps, and reachable nested plugin/channel config schema properties.
- What you did **not** verify: full suite, packaging, live gateway startup, or installed third-party extension behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] N/A; no bot review conversations were handled in this metadata draft.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: internal code that expects these generated `properties` maps to inherit `Object.prototype` could behave differently.
  - Mitigation: schema traversal in the touched path uses static object helpers and enumerable entries; JSON serialization remains unchanged for consumers that receive the schema over the wire.
target.author: mmaps

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
